This Consent to the Use of Electronic Records and Signatures (this "Consent") is provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC ("we," "us," or the "Lender") to Devin
Monroe ("you") in connection with loan number 1698754804 and the property located at 8757 Castillo Plain, Martinezburgh, MH 34647. Federal and applicable

§ 1. SCOPE OF YOUR CONSENT
Your consent applies to all records and communications that we, our affiliates, our service providers, and our successors and assigns are required by law to provide or make
available to you in writing, as well as to any other records and communications we elect to provide electronically in connection with your loan, your application, and any
related products or services. This includes, without limitation, application disclosures, loan estimates, closing disclosures, account statements, periodic notices, privacy
notices, tax and insurance communications, change-in-terms notices, and any amendments or supplements to the foregoing. Collectively, these are referred to in this
Consent as "Communications."
By consenting, you agree that we may satisfy any legal obligation to deliver a Communication to you in writing by delivering that Communication electronically.
Electronic delivery may occur by transmitting the Communication to the email address you have provided, by posting the Communication to a secure website or online
portal and notifying you that it is available, by displaying or delivering it through a mobile application, or by another electronic method reasonably designed to provide the
Communication to you. Your consent remains effective for the duration of your relationship with us unless and until withdrawn as described below.
This Consent does not require you to receive every Communication electronically, and we reserve the right, in our sole discretion, to deliver any particular Communication
to you on paper or to require that a given document be signed in ink, notwithstanding your consent. Where we elect to communicate on paper, we will use the most recent
postal address reflected in our records, and such delivery will not affect the continuing validity of your electronic consent for other Communications.
§ 2. ELECTRONIC SIGNATURES
You agree that your electronic signature on any application, agreement, disclosure acknowledgment, or other document presented to you in electronic form has the same
force and legal effect as a handwritten signature executed with pen and ink. An electronic signature may include, among other methods, clicking an "I Agree," "Accept,"
"Sign," or similar button, typing your name into a designated signature field, applying a stylus or finger-drawn mark, or otherwise adopting an electronic symbol or process
with the intent to sign the associated record. You agree not to contest the admissibility, validity, or enforceability of any such electronically signed record solely on the basis
that it was created, signed, or delivered electronically.
When you apply an electronic signature, you represent that you are the person identified in the signature block, that you are authorized to sign in the capacity indicated, and
that you intend to be legally bound by the contents of the document. You further acknowledge that an electronically executed document, together with any associated audit
trail, timestamp, system log, or authentication record maintained by us or our service providers, may be used as evidence of the existence and terms of your agreement in
any administrative or judicial proceeding to the same extent as an original paper document bearing an original signature.
§ 3. HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS
To access, view, and retain Communications delivered electronically, you must have, at a minimum, a personal computer or mobile device with internet access; a current
and supported web browser capable of supporting industry-standard encryption; software that enables you to view, save, and print documents in Portable Document Format
(PDF) or comparable file formats; sufficient electronic storage capacity or the ability to print and retain copies for your records; and a valid email account with an active
email address that is capable of sending and receiving electronic messages. You are solely responsible for obtaining, installing, maintaining, and operating the equipment,

From time to time, the hardware or software required to access, view, or retain Communications may change as technology, security standards, or applicable law evolve. If
our requirements change in a manner that creates a material risk that you will not be able to access or retain a Communication you previously consented to receive
electronically, we will notify you of the revised requirements and afford you the opportunity to withdraw your consent without the imposition of any fee, condition, or
consequence that was not previously disclosed to you. Your continued use of electronic Communications following such notice constitutes your reaffirmation of this
Consent.
§ 4. CONFIRMING YOUR ABILITY TO ACCESS
Before you consent to receive Communications electronically, you should confirm that you are able to access information in the electronic form that will be used to deliver
those Communications. You may be asked to demonstrate this ability by viewing this Consent on your device and successfully opening, displaying, and printing or saving a
sample document presented in the same format we will use. By providing your consent electronically, you confirm that your system meets the requirements described
above and that you have been able to access, read, and retain this Consent and any accompanying sample document.
If you do not provide consent, or if you later determine that you are unable to access Communications electronically, you may continue to receive Communications on
paper at no additional charge attributable solely to your election to receive paper, and your decision will not affect the terms of your loan or your eligibility for any product
or service for which you would otherwise qualify.
§ 5. OBTAINING PAPER COPIES
Even after you consent to electronic delivery, you retain the right to request and receive a paper copy of any Communication that we have provided or made available to
you electronically. To request a paper copy, you may contact us by telephone at 001-350-613-9200 or by writing to us at 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384, Karenview, NJ 46387,

§ 7. WITHDRAWING YOUR CONSENT

Withdrawing your consent will not invalidate or affect the legal effectiveness, validity, or enforceability of any Communication or electronically signed document that was

§ 8. FEDERAL AND STATE LAW GOVERNING THIS CONSENT
This Consent and the electronic delivery of Communications and the use of electronic signatures contemplated by it are made pursuant to, and are intended to comply with,
the federal Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act and, where applicable, the version of the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act or comparable

Nothing in this Consent is intended to require electronic delivery or electronic signing of any record where applicable law expressly prohibits delivery or execution in

§ 9. RECORDS RETENTION AND RELIABILITY
We will retain electronic records of Communications and of documents you sign electronically in a form that accurately reflects the information contained in each record

ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC — NOTICE OF SERVICING TRANSFER LENDER Rocket Mortgage, LLC
Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing
LOAN NO. 1698754804
Rights DATE 02/22/2024
BORROWER Devin Monroe
Issued to devIn Monroe
NOTICE & DISCLOSURE
This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to Devin Monroe in connection with loan number 1698754804 secured by the
property located at 8757 Castillo Plain, Martinezburgh, MH 34647, in accordance with the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and its implementing
regulation. The servicing of your mortgage loan, meaning the right to collect your periodic payments and to administer your loan, is being or may be transferred.
This Notice explains what servicing means, what the transfer does and does not change, where to direct your payments, how the sixty (60) day late-fee protection
period operates, and how to submit error-resolution notices and information requests. Please read it carefully and retain it with your permanent loan records.
§ 1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE
You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and process your scheduled periodic payments of principal, interest,
and any escrowed amounts, is being assigned, sold, or transferred. The transfer of servicing rights is a routine event in the residential mortgage industry and reflects an
arrangement between the prior servicer and Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This Notice is furnished pursuant to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act and applicable federal
regulation, which require that affected borrowers be informed when responsibility for the day-to-day administration of a federally related mortgage loan moves from one
servicer to another.
Nothing contained in this Notice should be read as a solicitation, an offer to refinance, or a demand for any payment other than the amounts already due under your existing
mortgage documents. The information presented here is intended solely to identify the parties involved in the servicing transfer, to specify the effective dates relevant to
that transfer, and to describe the rights and continuing obligations of Devin Monroe during and after the transition. If anything in this Notice appears to conflict with the
terms of your promissory note or security instrument, those underlying loan documents, except as expressly modified, continue to govern your loan.
§ 2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS
The term servicing refers to the administrative handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is the entity responsible for collecting your monthly principal and
interest payments, managing any escrow account established for the payment of property taxes and hazard or other required insurance, applying payments to your account,
issuing periodic statements and year-end tax information, responding to your inquiries, and otherwise administering the loan on behalf of the holder of the mortgage. The
servicer may or may not be the same entity that owns the underlying mortgage debt, and ownership of the loan and the right to service it are distinct interests that may be
held separately.
When servicing is transferred, the entity to which Devin Monroe directs payments and account questions changes, but the financial terms of the loan itself do not. The
interest rate, the unpaid principal balance, the maturity date, the payment schedule, and all other contractual provisions set forth in your promissory note and security
instrument remain in full force and effect. A transfer of servicing is therefore administrative in nature: it changes who collects and processes your payments and to whom
you address correspondence, while leaving intact the legal obligations that you assumed when you executed your loan documents.
§ 3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING
Effective on or about the transfer date identified in the accompanying loan documents, the servicing of your mortgage loan will be handled by Rocket Mortgage, LLC. The
prior servicer will cease to accept payments on your loan as of the close of business on the day immediately preceding the transfer date, and Rocket Mortgage, LLC will
begin accepting payments on the transfer date. You should have received, or will shortly receive, a separate notice from your prior servicer confirming the date on which it
will stop servicing your loan. Together, these two notices constitute the disclosure required by federal law in connection with a transfer of servicing.
If for any reason you did not receive a notice from your prior servicer, or if the dates described in the two notices do not appear to align, you should not assume that any
payment obligation has lapsed. Continue to make your scheduled payments to the appropriate servicer based on the effective transfer date, and contact Rocket Mortgage,
LLC at 001-350-613-9200 if you have questions regarding which entity should receive a particular payment. Maintaining your regular payment schedule throughout the
transition is essential to keeping your account current and avoiding the reporting of any delinquency.
§ 4. IDENTITY OF YOUR NEW SERVICER
Your new servicer is Rocket Mortgage, LLC, and all payments, inquiries, and written correspondence concerning the servicing of your loan should be directed to it on and
after the transfer date. The business address for Rocket Mortgage, LLC is 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384, Karenview, NJ 46387, and its toll-free or designated telephone
number for borrower assistance is 001-350-613-9200. Representatives are generally available during normal business hours to answer questions about your account, your
payment options, your escrow account, and the transition of servicing. When you contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC, please reference loan number 1698754804 so that your
account can be located promptly.
Rocket Mortgage, LLC is committed to a seamless transition and to honoring the terms of your existing loan. The records, payment history, and escrow information
associated with your loan are being transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC so that it may administer the loan without interruption. If you have established automatic
payment arrangements, recurring electronic drafts, or bill-payment instructions with your prior servicer, you should confirm with Rocket Mortgage, LLC whether those
arrangements have carried over or whether you must re-establish them, as such arrangements do not always transfer automatically between servicers.
§ 5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS
Beginning on the transfer date, you must send your mortgage payments to Rocket Mortgage, LLC rather than to your prior servicer. Payments may be remitted to the
address designated by Rocket Mortgage, LLC for the receipt of payments, which will be set forth in your welcome correspondence and on your periodic billing statements;
general correspondence and account questions may be directed to Rocket Mortgage, LLC at 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384, Karenview, NJ 46387. To ensure that your
payments are credited promptly and to the correct account, always include loan number 1698754804 on your check, money order, or electronic remittance, and follow any
specific remittance instructions provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

Payments received by your prior servicer after the transfer date will ordinarily be forwarded to Rocket Mortgage, LLC, but you should not rely on such forwarding as a
routine method of payment. To avoid delays in the crediting of your account, send all payments due on or after the transfer date directly to Rocket Mortgage, LLC. If you
remit payment through a third-party bill-payment service or your own financial institution, promptly update the payee name, address, and account number on file with that
service so that your payments are routed to Rocket Mortgage, LLC for all installments coming due on and after the effective date of the transfer.
§ 6. SIXTY-DAY LATE FEE PROTECTION
Federal law provides important protection to borrowers during a transfer of servicing. During the sixty (60) day period beginning on the effective date of the transfer of
servicing, a payment received by your prior servicer on or before its due date, or within any applicable grace period, may not be treated as late by either servicer, and no
late fee or other penalty may be imposed on you, and no delinquency may be reported to any consumer reporting agency, solely because the payment was sent to the prior
servicer rather than to Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This protection is intended to guard against penalties that could otherwise arise from confusion about where to send
payments during the transition.
To benefit from this protection, you must still make each payment in full and on time according to the schedule in your loan documents; the sixty (60) day window protects
you only against being penalized for sending an otherwise timely payment to the wrong servicer, and it does not excuse a payment that is missed, made late, or made for
less than the amount due. After the sixty (60) day period has elapsed, all payments must be made to Rocket Mortgage, LLC in accordance with your loan documents, and
standard late charges and credit-reporting practices will apply to any payment that is not timely received by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.
§ 7. YOUR CONTINUING PAYMENT OBLIGATION
The transfer of servicing does not affect, reduce, suspend, or otherwise alter your obligation to make timely payments under your promissory note and security instrument.
Devin Monroe remains fully responsible for paying all amounts due, including principal, interest, and any required escrow deposits, on or before each scheduled due date,
regardless of which entity is servicing the loan at the time a payment comes due. The amount of your monthly payment, the application of payments to your account, and
all other contractual terms continue to be governed by your existing loan documents except to the extent they may be modified in a separate writing signed by the
appropriate parties.
If at any time you are unable to make a scheduled payment, you should contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC promptly at 001-350-613-9200 to discuss the options that may be
available to you. Do not allow uncertainty arising from the servicing transfer to cause you to withhold or delay a payment, as the protections described in this Notice are
limited and do not relieve you of your underlying obligation to pay. Keeping your account current throughout and after the transition is the most reliable way to preserve
your standing under the loan and to avoid the accrual of late charges or other consequences of delinquency.
§ 8. ESCROW ACCOUNT AND INSURANCE
If your loan includes an escrow account for the payment of property taxes, hazard insurance premiums, mortgage insurance, or other charges, the administration of that
account will transfer to Rocket Mortgage, LLC along with the servicing of your loan. Rocket Mortgage, LLC will continue to collect escrow deposits as part of your
monthly payment and to disburse funds from the escrow account to pay covered items as they come due. Your escrow account balance, payment history, and any cushion
permitted by applicable law will be transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC, and an annual escrow account statement will be furnished to you in accordance with applicable
requirements.
You should review your insurance policies and tax bills to confirm that Rocket Mortgage, LLC is correctly identified, where applicable, as the party to receive notices, and
you should forward to Rocket Mortgage, LLC any tax bills or insurance notices that you receive directly. Maintaining required hazard and, if applicable, flood insurance on
the property located at 8757 Castillo Plain, Martinezburgh, MH 34647 remains your obligation under the security instrument. If required coverage lapses, Rocket
Mortgage, LLC may obtain lender-placed insurance as permitted by your loan documents and applicable law, the cost of which may be charged to you and may be more
expensive and provide less coverage than a policy you obtain on your own.
§ 9. NOTICES OF ERROR AND INFORMATION REQUESTS
If you believe an error has occurred in the servicing of your loan, or if you need information about your loan, you have the right to submit a written notice of error or a
written request for information to Rocket Mortgage, LLC. To be effective, such a notice or request should be sent in writing to the address that Rocket Mortgage, LLC
designates for the receipt of error notices and information requests, which may differ from the address used for payments; that designated address will be identified in your
statements and welcome materials, with general correspondence directed to 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384, Karenview, NJ 46387. Your written communication should include
your name, loan number 1698754804, and sufficient detail to enable Rocket Mortgage, LLC to identify the account and the specific error or information at issue.
Upon receiving a qualifying written notice of error or information request, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will provide a written acknowledgment within the time required by
applicable law and will conduct a reasonable investigation or provide the requested information, or an explanation of why the information is unavailable, within the
applicable response period. While a covered error notice is being investigated, Rocket Mortgage, LLC may not furnish adverse information to a consumer reporting agency
regarding any payment that is the subject of the notice, to the extent required by law. Telephone calls to customer service may help resolve routine questions, but to invoke
these specific statutory protections you must submit your notice or request in writing.
§ 10. COMMUNICATIONS AND ACCOUNT INFORMATION
On and after the transfer date, Rocket Mortgage, LLC will send your periodic billing statements, escrow statements, year-end tax and interest summaries, and other
required disclosures relating to the servicing of your loan. You should expect to receive a welcome package or similar correspondence from Rocket Mortgage, LLC that
confirms your account number, payment address, due date, and the methods available for making payments and obtaining account information. Please review all such
communications carefully, retain them with your records, and notify Rocket Mortgage, LLC promptly of any discrepancy between the information shown and your
understanding of the terms of your loan.
To help ensure that you receive important notices without interruption, keep your mailing address, telephone number, and any email address on file with Rocket Mortgage,
LLC current at all times. If you have authorized electronic delivery of statements or other documents, confirm that your electronic delivery preferences have been carried
over to Rocket Mortgage, LLC or re-establish them as necessary. Should you have any question about the authenticity of a communication that appears to come from
Rocket Mortgage, LLC, you may verify it by contacting Rocket Mortgage, LLC directly at 001-350-613-9200 before providing any personal or financial information in
response.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

§ 11. EFFECT ON LOAN TERMS AND LEGAL RIGHTS
Except for the change in the identity of the servicer and the place to which payments and correspondence are directed, the transfer of servicing has no effect on the terms or
conditions of your mortgage loan other than those directly related to the servicing of the loan. Your promissory note, your security instrument, and any riders, addenda, or
modifications thereto remain binding according to their terms. The transfer does not constitute a refinancing, a novation, or a waiver of any right or remedy available to the
holder of the loan or to Devin Monroe, and it does not enlarge or diminish any defense or claim that either party may otherwise possess.
This Notice is provided for informational purposes and to satisfy applicable disclosure obligations; it is not legal or financial advice. If you have questions about how the
transfer of servicing affects your particular circumstances, or about your rights and obligations under federal or MH law, you may wish to consult an attorney or a qualified
housing counselor. Rocket Mortgage, LLC will administer your loan in accordance with the loan documents and applicable law, and it will make available to you, upon
reasonable request, information necessary to understand the status and handling of your account following the transfer.

Schedule of Fees and Charges
LOAN 1698754804 / ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC / 02/22/2024
This Schedule of Fees and Charges is furnished by Rocket Mortgage, LLC ("Lender," "we," "us,"
or "our") to Devin Monroe ("Borrower," "you," or "your") in connection with the loan identified as
1698754804 and secured by the property located at 8757 Castillo Plain, Martinezburgh, MH
34647. It describes the categories of fees, service charges, and reimbursable advances that
may be assessed in the servicing of your account under applicable law and the terms of your
note and security instrument. This Schedule is provided for informational purposes, does not
modify the loan documents, and should be read together with them. Fees described here may
not all apply to your transaction, and additional charges permitted by law may be assessed as
circumstances require.
1. GENERAL FEE TERMS
The fees and charges summarized in this Schedule are imposed in accordance with the terms of your
note, your security instrument, and applicable federal and MH law. Not every fee described will apply to
your loan; whether a particular charge is assessed depends on the activity occurring on your account,
the services you request, and conditions affecting the property. Where a fee is authorized by your loan
documents but not specifically enumerated here, that authorization continues to govern. We may assess
any fee permitted by law and contract at the time the related service is performed or the related event
occurs, and such fees become part of the amount owed on your account.
Unless otherwise required by law or expressly stated in your loan documents, fees are earned when the
corresponding service is rendered and are generally nonrefundable. Certain fees reflect amounts charged
to us by third parties, including governmental authorities, insurers, attorneys, inspectors, and other
service providers, and such amounts may vary by provider, jurisdiction, and the nature of the work
performed. We reserve the right to advance these amounts on your behalf and to seek reimbursement as
provided in your security instrument. Any fee collected in excess of the amount permitted by law will be
applied or refunded as required by applicable law.
This Schedule is provided as a convenience and a general guide; it is not an exhaustive list of every
charge that may arise during the life of your loan. The absence of a particular fee from this Schedule
does not waive our right to assess it where authorized. To the extent any provision of this Schedule
conflicts with your note, your security instrument, or applicable law, the controlling document or the law
governs. We will furnish more specific fee information upon request, subject to any charge for documents
disclosed elsewhere in this Schedule.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

2. RETURNED PAYMENT CHARGES
If any payment, instrument, or other tender submitted toward your account is returned, dishonored,
reversed, or otherwise not honored by the financial institution on which it is drawn, for any reason, we
may assess a returned-item charge in the maximum amount permitted by applicable MH law and by your
loan documents. This charge applies regardless of the form of the tender, including checks, drafts,
electronic funds transfers, and automated clearing house entries, and regardless of whether the dishonor
results from insufficient funds, a closed or frozen account, a stop-payment order, or other cause.
A returned payment does not constitute payment of the installment to which it was directed, and the
related installment will remain due and unpaid until satisfied by good funds. Consequently, a returned
payment may also result in the assessment of a late charge if the substitute payment is not received
within the applicable grace period, and may affect the reporting of your account to credit bureaus.
Following a returned item, we may, to the extent permitted by law, require that future payments be made
by certified funds, cashier's check, money order, or another guaranteed method for a reasonable period.
The returned-item charge is intended to compensate us for the administrative costs and processing
expense associated with handling a dishonored tender and is in addition to, and not in lieu of, any other
remedy available to us. Where applicable law establishes a maximum dollar amount or limits the
frequency with which such a charge may be imposed, we will observe that limit. Returned-item charges,
once assessed, are due and payable and may be added to the balance of your account if not paid
separately.
3. LATE CHARGES
Your note establishes the date on which each periodic payment is due and the grace period, if any, within
which payment may be received without a late charge. If the full amount of a scheduled payment is not
received by the end of the applicable grace period, we may assess a late charge calculated and limited in
the manner set forth in your note and as permitted by applicable MH and federal law. The late charge
compensates us for the added expense of handling a delinquent payment and does not represent
interest on the overdue amount.
A late charge is assessed with respect to a delinquent installment and is not affected by the subsequent
receipt of that installment; payment of the past-due installment after the grace period does not relieve the
obligation to pay the late charge that has already accrued. Partial payments that do not equal the full
amount of a scheduled installment may not prevent the assessment of a late charge, and we are not
obligated to accept a partial payment except as required by your loan documents or applicable law.
Multiple consecutive delinquencies may result in late charges on each affected installment.
Late charges are due when assessed and may be collected from subsequent payments to the extent
permitted by the payment-application terms of your loan documents and applicable law, or added to the
unpaid balance of your account. We may, in our discretion and without obligation, waive a late charge in
an individual instance; any such waiver applies only to the specific charge waived and does not establish
a course of dealing or waive our right to assess late charges on other delinquent installments.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

4. PAYOFF AND DEMAND STATEMENT FEES
Upon request, we will prepare and furnish a statement setting forth the amount required to pay your loan
in full as of a specified date, commonly referred to as a payoff statement, demand statement, or
beneficiary statement. Where permitted by applicable MH law and your loan documents, we may charge
a reasonable fee for the preparation and delivery of such a statement, including any fee authorized for
delivery by facsimile, expedited mail, or other expedited means. Certain jurisdictions require that an initial
payoff statement be provided without charge or within a prescribed period, and we will comply with any
such requirement.
A payoff statement reflects amounts due as of the stated good-through date and is contingent upon the
receipt of good funds on or before that date. Because interest, fees, advances, and other charges may
continue to accrue, a payoff statement may include a per-diem interest figure and may expire if payment
is not received by the good-through date, in which event an updated statement may be required and an
additional preparation fee may apply. Amounts shown in a payoff statement remain subject to final
verification, including the clearance of recent payments and the resolution of any pending charges.
We may also charge reasonable fees, to the extent permitted by law, for the preparation and recording of
documents necessary to release, reconvey, or satisfy the lien securing your loan upon payment in full, as
well as for any recording, courier, or governmental charge incurred in connection with such release. Fees
associated with lien release and reconveyance, where assessable, are typically disclosed at or before
payoff and may be required to be collected as part of the payoff amount in accordance with applicable
MH law.
5. OPTIONAL EXPEDITED PAYMENT SERVICES
As a convenience, we may from time to time offer optional services that allow you to make a payment by
telephone, through an online portal, or through a customer service representative, with same-day or
expedited processing. These services are entirely optional. You are never required to use an expedited
payment service to make a timely payment, and you may always submit your payment without charge by
the standard methods described in your monthly statement and loan documents, including by mail and
through scheduled automated transfers.
Where we offer an optional expedited payment service for which a fee is charged, the fee, the available
processing options, and the cutoff times for same-day crediting will be disclosed to you before you
authorize the transaction, and your authorization to proceed constitutes your agreement to the disclosed
fee. Such convenience fees compensate us for the cost of providing the expedited channel and are
separate from, and in addition to, the payment amount applied to your account. We will not impose an
expedited payment fee where prohibited by applicable law.
The availability, terms, and pricing of optional expedited payment services may change, and any such
service may be modified, suspended, or discontinued at our discretion without affecting your obligation
to make timely payments by other means. Using an expedited service does not alter the contractual due
date or grace period applicable to your loan; a payment made through an expedited channel is credited
in accordance with the payment-application terms of your loan documents and applicable law as of the
date the payment is actually received in good funds.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

6. PROPERTY INSPECTION AND PRESERVATION
If your loan becomes delinquent, or if the property securing your loan appears to be vacant, abandoned,
or inadequately maintained, we may, to the extent permitted by your security instrument and applicable
MH law, conduct or arrange property inspections to assess the condition and occupancy of the property.
The reasonable cost of each such inspection may be charged to your account. Inspections are
performed to protect the value of the property and our interest in it and do not relieve you of your
obligation to maintain the property as required by your loan documents.
Where the property is found to require protective measures, we may incur expenses to preserve and
protect it, which may include securing the premises, winterization, debris removal, repair of conditions
that threaten habitability or value, and similar property-preservation activities authorized by your security
instrument. The reasonable costs of such preservation efforts may be advanced by us and charged to
your account as amounts secured by the security instrument, bearing interest as and to the extent
provided in your loan documents.
Property inspection and preservation charges reflect amounts billed to us by third-party vendors or
governmental authorities and may vary with the location, condition, and circumstances of the property.
We will assess such charges only as permitted by applicable law and your loan documents, and we will
not charge for an inspection or preservation activity more frequently than is reasonably necessary to
protect the security for the loan.
7. FORCE-PLACED INSURANCE CHARGES
Your security instrument requires you to maintain hazard, flood, and any other insurance coverage
required by us on the property securing your loan. If you fail to maintain required coverage, or if you do
not provide satisfactory evidence of such coverage upon request, we may, after providing any notices
required by applicable law, purchase insurance to protect our interest in the property. This coverage is
commonly referred to as force-placed, lender-placed, or creditor-placed insurance, and the cost of such
coverage may be charged to you and added to the amount you owe.
Force-placed insurance is obtained solely to protect our interest in the property and may be substantially
more expensive than coverage you could obtain on your own. It may provide less protection than a
policy you select, may not cover your personal property or personal liability, and may not name you as an
insured beneficiary. We strongly encourage you to maintain your own adequate insurance at all times to
avoid the placement of, and charges for, lender-placed coverage. Upon receipt of satisfactory evidence
that you maintained acceptable coverage during the relevant period, we will cancel any overlapping
force-placed coverage and refund or credit charges as required by applicable law.
Premiums and related charges for force-placed insurance reflect amounts charged to us by the insurer
and applicable governmental charges, and such amounts may be advanced by us and treated as
amounts secured by the security instrument. We will provide the notices, timing, and disclosures required
by applicable federal and MH law before assessing, and in connection with, force-placed insurance
charges, and we will comply with all applicable limitations on the imposition of such charges.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

8. TAX-RELATED ADVANCES
Your loan documents obligate you to pay, when due, all real property taxes, special assessments, and
similar governmental charges levied against the property securing your loan. If such taxes or
assessments become delinquent and are not paid, or if a tax lien or sale threatens our interest in the
property, we may, as permitted by your security instrument and applicable MH law, advance funds to pay
the delinquent taxes, assessments, penalties, and related charges in order to protect the security for the
loan. Amounts so advanced may be charged to your account.
Tax-related advances reflect amounts paid by us to taxing authorities and may include penalties, interest,
and redemption costs imposed by those authorities, as well as reasonable service charges for the
research and processing necessary to identify and satisfy the obligation. Such advances are treated as
amounts secured by the security instrument and may bear interest as and to the extent provided in your
loan documents. Making a tax advance does not waive any default arising from your failure to pay taxes
when due, nor does it obligate us to make any further advance.
If your loan includes an escrow or impound account for the payment of taxes and insurance, the
administration of that account, including any shortage, surplus, or deficiency, is governed by the
separate escrow disclosures provided to you and by applicable law, including the Real Estate Settlement
Procedures Act where applicable. Tax-related advances described in this section generally arise where
there is no escrow account, where escrowed funds are insufficient, or where amounts otherwise remain
unpaid and threaten the priority or security of our lien.
9. DOCUMENT AND RESEARCH FEES
Upon your request, and to the extent permitted by applicable MH and federal law and your loan
documents, we may provide copies of loan documents, account histories, year-end statements,
amortization schedules, and similar records, and we may charge a reasonable fee for the research,
reproduction, and delivery of such materials. Certain documents and disclosures are provided without
charge as required by law, including statements and notices that we are obligated to furnish, and no fee
will be assessed where the law prohibits it or requires that the item be provided free of charge.
We may also charge reasonable fees for special services you request that fall outside the ordinary
servicing of your account, such as the preparation of subordination agreements, partial release
documents, modification documents, assumption processing, or responses to certain third-party
requests, in each case only to the extent the charge is authorized by your loan documents and applicable
law. The amount of any such fee may reflect the complexity of the request and amounts charged to us by
third parties, including attorneys, title companies, and recording offices.
Where a requested service requires payment to a governmental authority or other third party, such as a
recording fee or a charge for a title or lien search, the actual amount charged to us may be passed
through to you in addition to any service fee. We will, upon request and where reasonably practicable,
advise you of an applicable document or research fee before performing the service so that you may
decide whether to proceed.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

10. DEFAULT AND COLLECTION COSTS
In the event of a default under your note or security instrument, you may, to the extent permitted by
those documents and applicable MH and federal law, be responsible for the reasonable costs and
expenses we incur in enforcing our rights and protecting our interest in the property. Such costs may
include, without limitation, attorneys' fees, court costs, trustee's fees, title and lien search charges,
foreclosure and bankruptcy-related fees, and other expenses authorized by your loan documents and
permitted by law. These amounts may be added to the balance of your account as provided in your
security instrument.
Default-related charges reflect amounts billed to us by attorneys, trustees, courts, and other third parties,
and the nature and amount of such charges depend on the stage of any enforcement proceeding, the
jurisdiction in which the property is located, and the requirements of applicable law. We will assess such
charges only to the extent they are actually incurred, are reasonable, and are permitted by your loan
documents and applicable law, and we will observe any statutory limitations, notice requirements, and
reinstatement rights that apply.
Curing a default by bringing your account current, where a right to do so exists, may require payment of
accrued default and collection costs in addition to the past-due amounts, to the extent permitted by law
and your loan documents. Nothing in this section creates an obligation on our part to accept a partial
cure or to forbear from exercising any remedy, except as required by applicable law or by the terms of
your loan documents.
11. HOW FEES MAY CHANGE
The fees and charges described in this Schedule may change from time to time. Many of the amounts
reflect charges imposed on us by third parties or by governmental authorities, which we do not control
and which may increase or decrease without notice to us. Other fees may be adjusted to reflect changes
in our costs, changes in applicable law, or changes in the maximum amounts permitted by your loan
documents and by applicable MH and federal law. Where a fee is set by a controlling statute or
regulation, the amount we may charge will rise or fall with that legal limit.
We will provide advance notice of changes to fees where notice is required by applicable law or by your
loan documents, and any such change will take effect only as permitted by law and the governing
documents. The continued servicing of your account, and your continued use of optional services for
which a fee is disclosed, are subject to the fees in effect at the time the related service is performed or
the related event occurs. This Schedule may be amended or superseded; the most recent version made
available to you, together with your loan documents and applicable law, controls.
Nothing in this Schedule obligates us to charge any particular fee, and our decision not to assess a fee
on one occasion does not waive our right to assess that fee, or any other fee, on a later occasion. If you
have questions about a specific charge on your account, or about whether a fee applies to your loan, you
may contact us at 001-350-613-9200 or in writing at 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384, Karenview, NJ 46387,
and we will respond as required by applicable law.
Retain this document with your loan file records.

12. WAIVERS AND DISCRETION
We may, in our sole discretion and without obligation, reduce or waive a particular fee or charge in an
individual case, including as an accommodation in connection with a hardship, a payment arrangement,
or a loss-mitigation effort. Any such reduction or waiver applies only to the specific charge addressed
and only for the occasion on which it is granted. A waiver of one fee is not a waiver of any other fee, is
not a modification of your loan documents or this Schedule, and does not establish a custom, practice,
or course of dealing upon which you may rely with respect to future charges.
No waiver of any fee, charge, right, or remedy is effective unless and only to the extent we grant it, and
our acceptance of a late or partial payment, our forbearance in collecting a charge, or our delay in
exercising a right does not constitute a waiver of that charge or right or of our ability to enforce the full
terms of your loan documents. To the extent applicable law requires that a waiver be in writing or follow a
particular procedure, we will observe that requirement.
If you believe a fee has been assessed in error, or if you wish to request the waiver or review of a charge,
you may contact us at 001-350-613-9200 or submit your request in writing to 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384,
Karenview, NJ 46387, referencing loan 1698754804. We will review timely and properly directed inquiries
and will respond within the period and in the manner required by applicable law, correcting any error and
refunding or crediting any amount as required.

The Binding Arbitration Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between Dennis Monroe ("Borrower") and Rocket Mortgage, LLC ("Lender"). This Agreement is intended to provide important rights and obligations regarding how to resolve disputes, so please read it carefully. Unless you opt out, it will apply to disputes between you and Rocket Mortgage, including disputes arising out of or relating to this Agreement or any loan service or product related to it. If you do not opt out, you will be giving up your right to go to court and have your disputes resolved by a judge or jury. Please read this Agreement carefully and keep it for your records. Capitalized terms have the meanings given to them in the Agreement.

1. AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE:
You and Rocket Mortgage, LLC agree that any dispute that otherwise would be resolved in this Agreement, any Civil Dispute, shall be resolved by neutral, binding arbitration. The arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with the arbitration rules of the American Arbitration Association. The Arbitrator’s decision shall be final and binding. This arbitration provision shall survive termination of this Agreement. The parties understand that a discovery period allows each party to request documents and ask questions of the other party to better prepare for arbitration. The parties further understand that the Federal Arbitration Act, 9 U.S.C. Sec. 1 et seq. (the "FAA"), governs the arbitration process, and applicable state law governs all other aspects of this Agreement. The arbitration shall be held at a location reasonably convenient to the parties.

2. DISPUTES COVERED:
This Agreement applies to any claim, dispute, or controversy of any kind, whether based on contract, tort, statute, regulation, common law, equity, or otherwise, that arises out of or relates to this Agreement, the property covered by the Agreement, or Rocket Mortgage, LLC.

The parties agree that any dispute shall be submitted to arbitration.

The term "Claim" includes, but is not limited to, any claim, dispute, or controversy arising out of or related to any transaction or relationship between the parties.

3. DISPUTES EXCLUDED:
Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement does not require arbitration of the following types of disputes:
 a) disputes that arise from the residential mortgage being made by Rocket Mortgage, LLC (or other lenders, including, but not limited to, disputes regarding the payment amount, fees, charges, and other costs related to the mortgage);
 b) disputes that arise from a violation of applicable federal, state, or local law; or
 c) disputes related to any foreclosures.

4. SMALL-CLAIMS CARVE-OUT:
Either you or Rocket Mortgage, LLC may elect to bring and pursue your claims in a small-claims court when appropriate, provided that such claims are within the scope of the small-claims court's jurisdiction, provided that such proceeding is completed within the time limits and in accordance with the court's rules, but only if the dispute is not transferred, removed, or appealed. Any such small-claims action must be between you and Rocket Mortgage, LLC only. Nothing in this Agreement limits your right to seek relief in a small-claims court as allowed by applicable law. The arbitration provision set forth herein shall not apply to the small-claims action.

A claim brought in small claims court must be asserted only by the claimant's individual capacity and may not be removed to, or consolidated with, any other actions. The Class Action Waiver applies unless and until the arbitration clause is made unenforceable.

8. ADMINISTERING BODY AND RULES  
Unless the parties agree in writing to another process, a Covered Dispute shall be administered by a nationally recognized, and well-established Arbitration organization. The arbitration shall be governed by the rules of such Arbitration organization, and to the extent such rules conflict with the arbitration provisions of this Agreement, those provisions shall control. You can obtain information regarding the current rules of any Arbitration organization at their website or by contacting that organization directly.

The arbitrator shall have the authority to determine the arbitrability of any Covered Dispute and to resolve any disputes concerning the interpretation, applicability, enforceability, or formation of this Agreement, including but not limited to any claim that all or any part of this Agreement is void or voidable.

9. EFFECT OF ARBITRATION; JURY-TRIAL WAIVER  
BY AGREEING TO ARBITRATE, COVERED PARTIES, YOU AND ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC EXPRESSLY, VOLUNTARILY AND INTENTIONALLY WAIVE THE RIGHT TO A TRIAL BY JURY AND THE RIGHT TO HAVE COVERED DISPUTES DECIDED BY A COURT OF LAW, INCLUDING THE RIGHT TO APPEAL.

This means that any Covered Disputes that arise will be arbitrated, meaning the parties will give up their rights to litigate covered disputes, their rights to a jury trial, and the right to appeal decisions made by arbitrators.

Covered disputes shall be arbitrated only on an individual basis. You and Rocket Mortgage, LLC each waive any right to arbitrate or litigate any Covered Dispute as a class action, collective action, private attorney general action, or other representative action, or to participate in any such action. Claims must be arbitrated or litigated individually and not consolidated with other claims or proceedings.

12. COSTS AND FEES  
The party initiating the arbitration shall pay the filing fee required by the arbitration organization. Rocket Mortgage, LLC will reimburse any filing fees that exceed the amount of the filing fee required to file a lawsuit in the jurisdiction where the Claim is initiated. Rocket Mortgage, LLC will also pay any fees required by the arbitrator or arbitration organization. In the event a Claim is resolved before an arbitrator convenes a hearing, you agree to provide reasonable documentation of any such resolution to Rocket Mortgage, LLC.

The arbitrator shall have discretion to allocate costs and fees among the parties. You are entitled to recover the costs and fees you incur in connection with arbitration if you prevail in the arbitration.

9. LOCATION AND CONDUCT OF ARBITRATION  
Arbitration will take place in the county in which you reside or at a mutually agreed location.

1.  RIGHT TO OPT OUT  You have the right to request that we do not sell or share your personal information. To opt out, you can send your request to: M&T Bank, Attn: Privacy, 2500 W. Market St., York, PA 17404, or email us at Privacy@mtb.com. You are also entitled to certain access and deletion rights under applicable laws. For example, in some jurisdictions, you may have the right to see what personal information we have collected about you and the right to request that we delete it. To exercise these rights, please contact us using the information provided in the "Contact Us" section of our website.  2.  SEVERABILITY AND SURVIVAL  Except as otherwise provided with respect to the arbitration and improvement review in Section 1, if any provision of this Agreement is found to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, it shall be severed from this Agreement, and the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect, and the invalid provision shall be deemed modified to the extent necessary to render it valid and enforceable in accordance with applicable law. The provisions of this Agreement, including Sections 1 through 6, shall survive termination of this Agreement, including any statutory or other limitations.

CONTINGENCIES
(a) Financing Contingency. This Agreement is contingent upon BUYER obtaining a firm written mortgage loan commitment in the approximate amount of $741,756.00 at prevailing market rates. If BUYER is unable to obtain such loan commitment prior to the agreed-upon dead-line, SELLER may terminate this Agreement upon notice to BUYER.
(b) Inspection Contingency. This Agreement is contingent upon BUYER's right, at BUYER's expense, to inspect the Property and to have licensed inspectors of BUYER'S choosing. Should inspection occur and should the results indicate material repairs, therefore, the right to negotiate repairs, if necessary, shall be the sole discretion of BUYER.
(c) Title Contingency. SELLER shall convey good and marketable title by warranty deed, free and clear of all encumbrances, except those of record acceptable to BUYER. Title shall be.

Insured through Fidelity National Title. BUYER may terminate this Agreement if title defects are not timely cured.

If BUYER defaults, SELLER may retain the earnest money as liquidated damages. If SELLER defaults, BUYER may pursue specific performance or recover the earnest money together with any actual damages permitted by law.

ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC — CONSENT TO ELECTRONIC RECORDS AND
SIGNATURES (E-SIGN DISCLOSURE)
Consent to the Use of Electronic Records and Signatures
Issued to devIn Monroe

LENDER Rocket Mortgage, LLC
LOAN NO. 1698754804
DATE 02/22/2024
BORROWER Devin Monroe

This Consent to the Use of Electronic Records and Signatures (this "Consent") is provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC ("we," "us," or the "Lender") to Devin
Monroe ("you") in connection with loan number 1698754804 and the property located at 8757 Castillo Plain, Martinezburgh, MH 34647. Federal and applicable
MH law permit us to deliver disclosures, agreements, notices, and other communications to you electronically and to obtain your signature in electronic form,
provided you affirmatively consent. Please read this Consent carefully and retain a copy for your records. By proceeding electronically, you acknowledge that you
have read, understood, and agreed to the terms set forth below as of February 22, 2024.
§ 1. SCOPE OF YOUR CONSENT
Your consent applies to all records and communications that we, our affiliates, our service providers, and our successors and assigns are required by law to provide or make
available to you in writing, as well as to any other records and communications we elect to provide electronically in connection with your loan, your application, and any
related products or services. This includes, without limitation, application disclosures, loan estimates, closing disclosures, account statements, periodic notices, privacy
notices, tax and insurance communications, change-in-terms notices, and any amendments or supplements to the foregoing. Collectively, these are referred to in this
Consent as "Communications."
By consenting, you agree that we may satisfy any legal obligation to deliver a Communication to you in writing by delivering that Communication electronically.
Electronic delivery may occur by transmitting the Communication to the email address you have provided, by posting the Communication to a secure website or online
portal and notifying you that it is available, by displaying or delivering it through a mobile application, or by another electronic method reasonably designed to provide the
Communication to you. Your consent remains effective for the duration of your relationship with us unless and until withdrawn as described below.
This Consent does not require you to receive every Communication electronically, and we reserve the right, in our sole discretion, to deliver any particular Communication
to you on paper or to require that a given document be signed in ink, notwithstanding your consent. Where we elect to communicate on paper, we will use the most recent
postal address reflected in our records, and such delivery will not affect the continuing validity of your electronic consent for other Communications.
§ 2. ELECTRONIC SIGNATURES
You agree that your electronic signature on any application, agreement, disclosure acknowledgment, or other document presented to you in electronic form has the same
force and legal effect as a handwritten signature executed with pen and ink. An electronic signature may include, among other methods, clicking an "I Agree," "Accept,"
"Sign," or similar button, typing your name into a designated signature field, applying a stylus or finger-drawn mark, or otherwise adopting an electronic symbol or process
with the intent to sign the associated record. You agree not to contest the admissibility, validity, or enforceability of any such electronically signed record solely on the basis
that it was created, signed, or delivered electronically.
When you apply an electronic signature, you represent that you are the person identified in the signature block, that you are authorized to sign in the capacity indicated, and
that you intend to be legally bound by the contents of the document. You further acknowledge that an electronically executed document, together with any associated audit
trail, timestamp, system log, or authentication record maintained by us or our service providers, may be used as evidence of the existence and terms of your agreement in
any administrative or judicial proceeding to the same extent as an original paper document bearing an original signature.
§ 3. HARDWARE AND SOFTWARE REQUIREMENTS
To access, view, and retain Communications delivered electronically, you must have, at a minimum, a personal computer or mobile device with internet access; a current
and supported web browser capable of supporting industry-standard encryption; software that enables you to view, save, and print documents in Portable Document Format
(PDF) or comparable file formats; sufficient electronic storage capacity or the ability to print and retain copies for your records; and a valid email account with an active
email address that is capable of sending and receiving electronic messages. You are solely responsible for obtaining, installing, maintaining, and operating the equipment,
software, and internet connectivity necessary to receive electronic Communications.
From time to time, the hardware or software required to access, view, or retain Communications may change as technology, security standards, or applicable law evolve. If
our requirements change in a manner that creates a material risk that you will not be able to access or retain a Communication you previously consented to receive
electronically, we will notify you of the revised requirements and afford you the opportunity to withdraw your consent without the imposition of any fee, condition, or
consequence that was not previously disclosed to you. Your continued use of electronic Communications following such notice constitutes your reaffirmation of this
Consent.
§ 4. CONFIRMING YOUR ABILITY TO ACCESS
Before you consent to receive Communications electronically, you should confirm that you are able to access information in the electronic form that will be used to deliver
those Communications. You may be asked to demonstrate this ability by viewing this Consent on your device and successfully opening, displaying, and printing or saving a
sample document presented in the same format we will use. By providing your consent electronically, you confirm that your system meets the requirements described
above and that you have been able to access, read, and retain this Consent and any accompanying sample document.
If you do not provide consent, or if you later determine that you are unable to access Communications electronically, you may continue to receive Communications on
paper at no additional charge attributable solely to your election to receive paper, and your decision will not affect the terms of your loan or your eligibility for any product
or service for which you would otherwise qualify.
§ 5. OBTAINING PAPER COPIES
Even after you consent to electronic delivery, you retain the right to request and receive a paper copy of any Communication that we have provided or made available to
you electronically. To request a paper copy, you may contact us by telephone at 001-350-613-9200 or by writing to us at 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384, Karenview, NJ 46387,

ROCKET MORTGAGE, LLC — NOTICE OF SERVICING TRANSFER
Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing
Rights
Issued to devIn Monroe

LENDER Rocket Mortgage, LLC
LOAN NO. 1698754804
DATE 02/22/2024
BORROWER Devin Monroe

This Notice of Assignment, Sale, or Transfer of Servicing Rights is provided to Devin Monroe in connection with loan number 1698754804 secured by the
property located at 8757 Castillo Plain, Martinezburgh, MH 34647, in accordance with the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act (RESPA) and its implementing
regulation. The servicing of your mortgage loan, meaning the right to collect your periodic payments and to administer your loan, is being or may be transferred.
This Notice explains what servicing means, what the transfer does and does not change, where to direct your payments, how the sixty (60) day late-fee protection
period operates, and how to submit error-resolution notices and information requests. Please read it carefully and retain it with your permanent loan records.
§ 1. PURPOSE OF THIS NOTICE
You are receiving this Notice because the servicing of your mortgage loan, that is, the right to receive and process your scheduled periodic payments of principal, interest,
and any escrowed amounts, is being assigned, sold, or transferred. The transfer of servicing rights is a routine event in the residential mortgage industry and reflects an
arrangement between the prior servicer and Rocket Mortgage, LLC. This Notice is furnished pursuant to the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act and applicable federal
regulation, which require that affected borrowers be informed when responsibility for the day-to-day administration of a federally related mortgage loan moves from one
servicer to another.
Nothing contained in this Notice should be read as a solicitation, an offer to refinance, or a demand for any payment other than the amounts already due under your existing
mortgage documents. The information presented here is intended solely to identify the parties involved in the servicing transfer, to specify the effective dates relevant to
that transfer, and to describe the rights and continuing obligations of Devin Monroe during and after the transition. If anything in this Notice appears to conflict with the
terms of your promissory note or security instrument, those underlying loan documents, except as expressly modified, continue to govern your loan.
§ 2. WHAT SERVICING MEANS
The term servicing refers to the administrative handling of your mortgage loan after closing. A servicer is the entity responsible for collecting your monthly principal and
interest payments, managing any escrow account established for the payment of property taxes and hazard or other required insurance, applying payments to your account,
issuing periodic statements and year-end tax information, responding to your inquiries, and otherwise administering the loan on behalf of the holder of the mortgage. The
servicer may or may not be the same entity that owns the underlying mortgage debt, and ownership of the loan and the right to service it are distinct interests that may be
held separately.
When servicing is transferred, the entity to which Devin Monroe directs payments and account questions changes, but the financial terms of the loan itself do not. The
interest rate, the unpaid principal balance, the maturity date, the payment schedule, and all other contractual provisions set forth in your promissory note and security
instrument remain in full force and effect. A transfer of servicing is therefore administrative in nature: it changes who collects and processes your payments and to whom
you address correspondence, while leaving intact the legal obligations that you assumed when you executed your loan documents.
§ 3. TRANSFER OF YOUR SERVICING
Effective on or about the transfer date identified in the accompanying loan documents, the servicing of your mortgage loan will be handled by Rocket Mortgage, LLC. The
prior servicer will cease to accept payments on your loan as of the close of business on the day immediately preceding the transfer date, and Rocket Mortgage, LLC will
begin accepting payments on the transfer date. You should have received, or will shortly receive, a separate notice from your prior servicer confirming the date on which it
will stop servicing your loan. Together, these two notices constitute the disclosure required by federal law in connection with a transfer of servicing.
If for any reason you did not receive a notice from your prior servicer, or if the dates described in the two notices do not appear to align, you should not assume that any
payment obligation has lapsed. Continue to make your scheduled payments to the appropriate servicer based on the effective transfer date, and contact Rocket Mortgage,
LLC at 001-350-613-9200 if you have questions regarding which entity should receive a particular payment. Maintaining your regular payment schedule throughout the
transition is essential to keeping your account current and avoiding the reporting of any delinquency.
§ 4. IDENTITY OF YOUR NEW SERVICER
Your new servicer is Rocket Mortgage, LLC, and all payments, inquiries, and written correspondence concerning the servicing of your loan should be directed to it on and
after the transfer date. The business address for Rocket Mortgage, LLC is 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384, Karenview, NJ 46387, and its toll-free or designated telephone
number for borrower assistance is 001-350-613-9200. Representatives are generally available during normal business hours to answer questions about your account, your
payment options, your escrow account, and the transition of servicing. When you contact Rocket Mortgage, LLC, please reference loan number 1698754804 so that your
account can be located promptly.
Rocket Mortgage, LLC is committed to a seamless transition and to honoring the terms of your existing loan. The records, payment history, and escrow information
associated with your loan are being transferred to Rocket Mortgage, LLC so that it may administer the loan without interruption. If you have established automatic
payment arrangements, recurring electronic drafts, or bill-payment instructions with your prior servicer, you should confirm with Rocket Mortgage, LLC whether those
arrangements have carried over or whether you must re-establish them, as such arrangements do not always transfer automatically between servicers.
§ 5. WHERE TO SEND YOUR PAYMENTS
Beginning on the transfer date, you must send your mortgage payments to Rocket Mortgage, LLC rather than to your prior servicer. Payments may be remitted to the
address designated by Rocket Mortgage, LLC for the receipt of payments, which will be set forth in your welcome correspondence and on your periodic billing statements;
general correspondence and account questions may be directed to Rocket Mortgage, LLC at 992 Adams Circle Apt. 384, Karenview, NJ 46387. To ensure that your
payments are credited promptly and to the correct account, always include loan number 1698754804 on your check, money order, or electronic remittance, and follow any
specific remittance instructions provided by Rocket Mortgage, LLC.
